title: rubus subintegribasis
intro: Rubus subintegribasis, also known as the fragrant raspberry, is a species of raspberry plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are used in traditional medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Rubus subintegribasis have been found to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and supporting digestion. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for topical and internal use, although its efficacy and safety may vary depending on the region and preparation method.
